channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Texas Blue Alert issued for suspect linked to Memphis officer shooting
item.description: A Blue Alert has been issued in Texas for Seth Altman, 33, who is wanted in connection with the shooting of a law enforcement officer in Memphis, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Holly Galvan Posey, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris and Trump battle for labor support as dockworkers suspend strike
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris will visit the union stronghold of Flint, Michigan, on Friday as she battles with Donald Trump for working-class voters who could tip the scales in this year's election. Her appearance in the battleground state comes the day after U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Japan's new leader expresses regret for governing party slush fund scandal, vows stronger defense
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — New Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba opened his first policy speech in office Friday by expressing deep regrets over the governing party's slush funds scandal that dogged his predecessor.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tunisia votes Sunday in its third presidential election since the Arab Spring
item.description: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — With his major opponents imprisoned or left off the ballot, Tunisian President Kais Saied faces few obstacles to winning reelection on Sunday, five years after riding anti-establishment backlash to a first term. The North African country's Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: International fiesta fills New Mexico's sky with colorful hot air balloons
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — One of the most photographed events in the world is set to kick off Saturday with a mass ascension of color for the 52nd annual Albuquerque International Balloon Fiesta.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: As search for Helene's victims drags into second week, sheriff says rescuers 'will not rest'
item.description: PENSACOLA, N.C. (AP) — The search for victims of Hurricane Helene dragged into its second week on Friday, as exhausted rescue crews and volunteers continued to work long days — navigating past washed out roads, downed power lines and mudslides — to reach the isolated and the missing.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Springsteen calls Trump 'most dangerous candidate for president in my lifetime,' backs Harris
item.description: The rock legend said the former president “doesn’t understand the meaning of this country, its history or what it means to be deeply American.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man cashes in $500K lottery ticket, finds another winning ticket for same payout a month later
item.description: A man from Washington, D.C., is another $500,000 richer after cashing in a second winning lottery ticket for a DC Lottery drawing in just as many months.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, WGN 9

item.title: American flag stands strong after tourist town leveled by remnants of Hurricane Helene: 'Hope and strength'
item.description: An American flag was placed on an iconic overlook in a North Carolina state park that was decimated after the powerful remnants of Hurricane Helene wrecked havoc on the town in the Blue Ridge Mountains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors to review new evidence in Menendez brothers' 1996 murder conviction
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prosecutors in Los Angeles are reviewing new evidence in the case of Erik and Lyle Menendez to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion more than 35 years ago, the city's district attorney said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Earth's 2nd moon has arrived: What you need to know
item.description: A second moon has officially entered Earth's orbit—sort of. Although it's being called a "mini-moon," it's actually an asteroid named 2024 PT5. The asteroid has been temporarily captured by Earth's gravitational pull. The asteroid will remain in Earth's orbit from now until Nov.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Lewis, KTLA 5

item.title: Oregon woman gets probation after freezing puppies to feed snake, gets to keep pets
item.description: An Oregon woman was sentenced on Wednesday on several animal abuse charges after authorities learned she froze several litters of puppies to feed her pet snakes.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 14 dogs from South Carolina arrive in Rhode Island after Hurricane Helene
item.description: "We are hoping that these dogs get adopted quickly so that we have some space so we can call them again and try to help them some more because they are dealing with so much," Potter League CEO Brad Shear said.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles DA George Gascon will review new evidence in Menendez brothers murder case
item.description: New evidence was presented to the Los Angeles County District Attorney's Office that needs to be reviewed in the Menendez brothers case, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man accused of trying to carjack pregnant woman in Connecticut drive-thru
item.description: With their help, police were able to arrest the suspect, who police say is facing a multitude of charges in this case and others.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, NewsNation

item.title: New video shows fallout from wild, violent street takeovers in Cleveland
item.description: Dozens of cars and huge crowds last weekend tied up traffic in Cleveland on the Innerbelt Bridge and on streets all over the city.
item.dc:creator: Ed Gallek, NewsNation

item.title: East Tennessee hospital employee recounts rooftop rescue during flood
item.description: "I think when you start looking at the data and seeing the magnitude of what happened, there's no predictability about that," said Harrah.
item.dc:creator: Alex Libby, NewsNation

item.title: Texas couple raffling ACL tickets to pay for costly endometriosis surgery
item.description: Cole Stepro first noticed symptoms in December 2023 and started a 10-month journey with 17 doctors across the country.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Girtman, NewsNation

item.title: Navy veteran student recounts saving his ASU classmate from stabbing attack: 'Compelled to do something'
item.description: An Arizona State University student sprang into action to help classmate during "random" stabbing attack on the campus, saying that he felt "compelled" to help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DC man cashes in $500K lottery ticket, finds another winning ticket for same payout
item.description: "Phillip was mum about his second winning ticket because he couldn't find it," according to the lottery's website.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Country music star Garth Brooks accused of sexual assault by makeup artist
item.description: Garth Brooks, the country music superstar who gained fame in the 1990s with several hit songs, has been named in a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Last pandemic-era student loan benefit ends even as borrowers struggle to make payments
item.description: The last of the pandemic-era student loan benefits are gone, winding down four years of additional help to borrowers that advocates say was never actually enough.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Helene scrambles politics in 3 battleground states
item.description: The 500-mile-long path of destruction cut by Hurricane Helene has scrambled the politics of three battleground states that could determine control of the White House and Senate: North Carolina, Georgia and Florida.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harris campaigns with Liz Cheney at the GOP's birthplace while Trump rallies in Michigan
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris has rallied side-by-side with Republican Liz Cheney in the birthplace of the modern Republican Party.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NYC mayor deflects questions about bribery charges as a potential witness speaks outside City Hall
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams delivered his most thorough public defense to date since his indictment on federal bribery charges, insisting the case was a “test” that would not distract him from governing as he deflected specific questions about the investigation for more than an ho
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: My car was submerged in floodwaters. Should I start it?
item.description: Storm surges and flooding from Hurricane Helene have caused widespread devastation across parts of the Southeastern U.S., in some cases leaving homes and cars completely underwater.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: A judge considers whether to clarify 2 new Georgia election certification rules
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A judge is considering whether to issue clarification or guidance for two new rules from the Georgia State Election Board that have to do with county certification of election results.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why Tuesday's vice presidential debate could matter more than history suggests
item.description: Tim Walz and JD Vance might have the chance to reshape the political landscape Tuesday in their first and only debate.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, ABC 4

item.title: Helene's death toll surpasses 150 as crews continue to search for survivors
item.description: Exhausted emergency workers continued to work around-the-clock to clear roads, restore power and cellphone service, and reach people left stranded by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kirk becomes a hurricane; expected to reach major hurricane strength this week
item.description: Tropical Storm Kirk is expected to become a hurricane Tuesday, and a major hurricane on Thursday, the National Hurricane Center said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: PlayStation Network reports global outage
item.description: PlayStation users are facing widespread outages across multiple platforms, leaving many unable to sign in or access online features.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department will launch civil rights review into 1921 Tulsa Race Massacre
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — The Justice Department announced Monday it plans to launch a review of the 1921 Tulsa Race Massacre, an attack by a white mob on a thriving Black district that is considered one of the worst single acts of violence against Black people in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI to pay $22M to settle claims of sexual discrimination at training academy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI agreed Monday to pay more than $22 million to settle a class-action lawsuit alleging female recruits were singled out for dismissal in training and routinely harassed by instructors with sexually charged comments about their breast size, false allegations of infidelity and
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump suggests ‘1 rough hour’ of policing would end retail crime
item.description: The former president called for law enforcement to be freed up to "do their job" without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Utah school district makes list of 50 best districts in America for 2025: Report
item.description: The rankings are based on ratings from current students, alumni, and parents, along with quantitative data from the U.S. Department of Education — and this district in Utah made the list.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: Submerged EVs can catch fire when inundated with saltwater: What to know
item.description: Electric vehicles can catch fire if they are inundated by saltwater, so owners who live in the path of a major storm should take precautions and prepare for the possibility that they'll be unable to charge their cars during a power outage.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5
